Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: First I need to remember what the standard Hire to Retire process is supposed to look like Let us start by recalling the normative flow first because that is the baseline we are comparing against

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard Hire to Retire flow logically First you post the job ad then you screen candidates then you conduct interviews then you make a hiring decision after interviews then if you hire someone you onboard them then add them to payroll then close the case Also all these steps are sequential in the standard case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First let us analyze Model 1 The edges are Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Post must come before Screen correct post ad first then screen Screen must come before Decide Screen must come before Interview Decide must come before Onboard Onboard before Payroll Payroll before Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: There is no edge between Decide and Interview So that means you could execute the Make Hiring Decision before Conduct Interviews because there is no edge saying Interview has to come before Decide

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That is a major flaw in Model 1 because logically you interview candidates before making a hiring decision

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now Model 2 Let us look at its components loop onboarding is a LOOP with Onboard and skip xor payroll is an XOR with Payroll and skip

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Post has edges to Screen and to Interview so Screen and Interview have no precedence relation between them

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That means you can conduct interviews before screening candidates which is a problem because logically you screen candidates first and then interview the ones who passed screening

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The XOR for payroll means either do Add to Payroll or skip it So adding the employee to payroll is optional

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: In a standard Hire to Retire process if you onboard a new employee you must add them to payroll Skipping payroll would mean the employee is not getting paid which is a major anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The loop on onboarding allows onboarding to be repeated multiple times which is not standard because onboarding is usually a one time activity for a new hire

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Model 2 Interview must happen before Decide because there is an edge from Interview to Decide which is the correct order

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 has only one anomaly Missing precedence between Interview and Decide All steps are mandatory and the order of the other steps is correct

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 has three anomalies Interview can be before Screen Payroll is optional and Onboarding can be repeated

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 requires only a single fix adding the Interview Decide precedence edge whereas Model 2 requires multiple fixes adding Screen Interview edge removing the XOR on payroll and removing the onboarding loop

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: A valid trace in Model 1 could be Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close That is a problem because you decide to hire before interviewing anyone

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: A valid trace in Model 2 could be Post Screen Interview Decide Onboard Close with payroll skipped That means the employee is not in payroll so the process is incomplete

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: A model that allows skipping a mandatory step is further from the standard than a model that includes all mandatory steps but has one ordering flaw

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 more closely aligns with the normative Hire to Retire process because all mandatory steps are executed in every trace there are no optional steps no unnecessary repetition and only one missing precedence constraint
